comments: Approved. The kickoff packet explicitly defines V0.7 as a new iteration, carries forward the three V0.6 items (state separation, legacy tooling deprecation, SubAgent review automation), includes the three new core requirements (multi-agent collaboration, governed self-evolution, environment discovery/adaptation), and preserves full kickoff-directive scope across the packet, milestone map, scope definition, requirements analysis, risk register, environment adaptation statement, and governance note. The materials also maintain the required governance distinctions: governed autonomy still requires independent review artifacts before self-modification or execution, low invasiveness is explicitly separated from protocol removal by preserving physically verified gates, environment adaptation is defined for multiple AI coding environments rather than a single toolchain, and high-risk migrations/deprecations are scoped as phased design-roadmap work rather than immediate execution.
